title: Homo Deus: A Brief History of Tomorrow
excerpt: A provocative vision of humanity's future — how the 21st century will see Homo sapiens strive for divinity, mastery over nature, and perhaps obsolescence.
keyIdeas: The three great human projects for the 21st century: immortality, happiness, divinity
Dataism as a new religion replacing humanism
The obsolescence of liberalism without the skills to back it up
keyTakeaways: The biggest 21st-century project is overcoming death
Liberalism is threatened by new technologies that make human decision-making obsolete
Dataism — the belief that the universe consists of data flows — may become the new dominant ideology
Upgrading humans into gods may create a new class of useless people
whoShouldRead: Anyone interested in the future of humanity and technology
Readers of Sapiens looking for the sequel
Students of history, AI ethics, and futurology
whoShouldSkip: Those easily alarmed by technological predictions
Readers seeking concrete policy proposals rather than big-picture speculation
